Tests if raising 1 to the power of NaN returns NaN using Math.pow.

# Purpose
This code is a test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.pow` function in JavaScript when the exponent is `NaN`. The `assert` function checks if the result of `Math.pow(1, NaN)` is `NaN`, which is the expected outcome according to the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case of the `Math.pow` function. It is useful for ensuring that the JavaScript environment correctly handles this mathematical operation.
